I COMPLETELY understand what Diana is saying especially about photographers that give you the worst edits back of yourself and will be complete babies about it and try and gatekeep the rest of your photos. It is such a struggle to find photographers that aren’t creeps or complete divas.

![Sofia Echa](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/0530cb3fe22c53e453aff46b329c4830?d=mm&s=70)

Sofia Echa

Awaiting Review 

5 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/11743885/comments/13729941)

I've had an experience with a great photographer who was professional during the whole shoot, almost didn't say a word, no weird comments, nothing like that. And then after we were done and I left, literally a minute after he started texting me very inappropriate stuff (I showed NO signs that it would be ok with me, no flirting, nothing). Before shooting with him I asked somebody who happened to shoot with him and they said he was super professional and great so I agreed. So many weirdos unfortunatelly.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

I like to hope that our culture will extinguish this toxic behavior for good one day. Thankfully there's more women than ever bringing their voice to photography.

![Pixie ](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/43eb684f805e9de257a508501cad715b?d=mm&s=70)

Pixie 

Awaiting Review 

5 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/11743885/comments/13290760)

I really liked the video with Diana. It was great seeing her transitions between poses. I loved that she highlighted the mindset of many creatives right now, "Controlling your image or reputation". People are becoming reluctant to collab because of this ego exchange of how someone wants to define you in the art they call theirs. The girl knows her strengths here. I see this in music collabs too. Eventually you feel the compromise isn't worth the energy exchange. So it is very rare to find someone who is actually at a point of working beyond ego, and rather in that place of listening, watching, harmonizing within the experience. Making it about the experience and not just the product. Capturing THAT vibe and not just an image. Thanks for the videos and perspectives as always x

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

Ashris Choudhury

Erika... giving us the inside story from the perspective I rarely hear. This is surely a public service -- I for one wish to treat and see people as human beings not just a "butt" or an object.
